name: scalecodec

metadata.name: scalecodec
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# Python SCALE Codec

[![Build Status](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/polkascan/py-scale-codec/unittests.yml?branch=master)](https://github.com/polkascan/py-scale-codec/actions/workflows/unittests.yml?query=workflow%3A%22Run+unit+tests%22)
[![Latest Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/scalecodec.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/scalecodec/) 
[![Supported Python versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/scalecodec.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/scalecodec/)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/scalecodec.svg)](https://github.com/polkascan/py-scale-codec/blob/master/LICENSE)


## Description
[Substrate](https://github.com/paritytech/substrate) uses a lightweight and efficient [encoding and decoding program](https://docs.substrate.io/reference/scale-codec/) to optimize how data is sent and received over the network. The program used to serialize and deserialize data is called the SCALE codec, with SCALE being an acronym for **S**imple **C**oncatenated **A**ggregate **L**ittle-**E**ndian.

## Documentation
https://polkascan.github.io/py-scale-codec/


## Installation
```bash
pip install scalecodec
```

## Examples of different types

| Type                                                                         | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Example SCALE decoding value                                                | SCALE encoded value                                                             |
|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `bool`                                                                       | Boolean values are encoded using the least significant bit of a single byte.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | `True`                                                                      | `0x01`                                                                          |
| `u16`                                                                        | Basic integers are encoded using a fixed-width little-endian (LE) format.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | `42`                                                                        | `0x2a00`                                                                        |
| `Compact`                                                                    | A "compact" or general integer encoding is sufficient for encoding large integers (up to 2**536) and is more efficient at encoding most values than the fixed-width version. (Though for single-byte values, the fixed-width integer is never worse.)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | `0`                                                                         | `0x00`                                                                          |
|                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | `1`                                                                         | `0x04`                                                                          |
|                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | `42`                                                                        | `0xa8`                                                                          |
|                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | `69`                                                                        | `0x1501`                                                                        |
|                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | `100000000000000`                                                           | `0x0b00407a10f35a`                                                              |
| `Vec`                                                                        | A collection of same-typed values is encoded, prefixed with a compact encoding of the number of items, followed by each item's encoding concatenated in turn.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | `[4, 8, 15, 16, 23, 42]`                                                    | `0x18040008000f00100017002a00`                                                  |
| `BitVec`                                                                     | A sequence of bools, represented in a more space efficient bit format                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | `0b00000010_01111101`                                                    | `0x287d02`                                                  |
| `str`,`Bytes`, `String`                                                      | Strings are Vectors of bytes (`Vec<u8>`) containing a valid UTF8 sequence.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | `"Test"`                                                                    | `0x1054657374`                                                                  |
|                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | `b"Test"`                                                                   | `0x1054657374`                                                                  |
|                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | `[84, 101, 115, 116]`                                                       | `0x1054657374`                                                                  |
| `[u8; 4]`                                                                    | Fixed sized array of in this case an `u8`                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | `b"babe"`                                                                   | `0x62616265`                                                                    |
|                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | `"0x62616265"`                                                              | `0x62616265`                                                                    |
|                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | `[98, 97, 98, 101]`                                                         | `0x62616265`                                                                    |
| `AccountId`                                                                  | An [SS58 formatted](https://docs.substrate.io/reference/address-formats/) representation of an account. See also the [SS58 util functions](https://polkascan.github.io/py-scale-codec/utils/ss58.html)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | `"5GDyPHLVHcQYPTWfygtPY eogQjyZy7J9fsi4brPhgEFq4pcv"`                       | `0xb80269ec500e458a630846b99105c397 ee574125823d6f4388e9c7572e115c05`           |
| `Enum` Example: `enum IntOrBool { Int(u8), Bool(bool),}`                     | A fixed number of variants, each mutually exclusive and potentially implying a further value or series of values. Encoded as the first byte identifying the index of the variant that the value is. Any further bytes are used to encode any data that the variant implies. Thus, no more than 256 variants are supported.                                                                                                                                                           | `{'Int': 8}`                                                                | `0x002a`                                                                        |
|                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | `{'Bool': True}`                                                            | `0x0101`                                                                        |
| `Struct` Example: `struct Motion { pub votes: Vec<AccountId>, pub id: u32 }` | For structures, the values are named, but that is irrelevant for the encoding (names are ignored - only order matters). All containers store elements consecutively. The order of the elements is not fixed, depends on the container, and cannot be relied on at decoding. This implicitly means that decoding some byte-array into a specified structure that enforces an order and then re-encoding it could result in a different byte array than the original that was decoded. | `{"votes": ["5GDyPHLVHcQYPTWfygtPYeo gQjyZy7J9fsi4brPhgEFq4pcv"], "id": 4}` | `0x04b80269ec500e458a630846b99105c397ee57 4125823d6f4388e9c7572e115c0504000000` |

functions.name: scalecodec.utils.ss58.ss58_decode
documentation:
Decodes given SS58 encoded address to an account ID
Parameters
----------
address: e.g. EaG2CRhJWPb7qmdcJvy3LiWdh26Jreu9Dx6R1rXxPmYXoDk
valid_ss58_format

Returns
-------
Decoded string AccountId

documentation:
Encodes an account ID to an Substrate address according to provided address_type

Parameters
----------
address
ss58_format

Returns
-------
str

functions.name: scalecodec.utils.ss58.is_valid_ss58_address
documentation:
Checks if given value is a valid SS58 formatted address, optionally check if address is valid for specified
ss58_format

Parameters
----------
value: value to checked
valid_ss58_format: if valid_ss58_format is provided the address must be valid for specified ss58_format (network) as well

Returns
-------
bool

functions.name: scalecodec.utils.ss58.get_ss58_format
documentation:
Returns the SS58 format for given SS58 address

Parameters
----------
ss58_address

Returns
-------
int

functions.name: scalecodec.utils.math.trailing_zeros
documentation:
Returns the number of trailing zeros in the binary representation of
the given integer.

functions.name: scalecodec.utils.math.next_power_of_two
documentation:
Returns the smallest power of two that is greater than or equal
to the given integer.

documentation:
Creates a new `ScaleType` object with given type_string, for example 'u32', 'Bytes' or 'scale_info::2'
(scale_info:: prefixed types are defined in the `PortableRegistry` object of the runtime metadata.)

Parameters
----------

data: ScaleBytes data to decode
kwargs

Returns
-------

block_number

Returns

Container for runtime configuration, for example type definitions and runtime upgrade information

sub_type

length: amount of requested bytes

documentation:
Retrieves the next byte and convert to an int

int

Retrieves the next byte and convert to an bool

bool

Retrieves all remaining bytes from the stream

bytearray

documentation:
Returns a bytearray of all SCALE-bytes used in the decoding process

Returns

bytearray

methods.name: scalecodec.base.ScaleDecoder.process
documentation:
Implementation of the decoding process

Returns
-------

methods.name: scalecodec.base.ScaleDecoder.decode
documentation:
Decodes available SCALE-bytes according to type specification of this ScaleType

If no `data` is provided, it will try to decode data specified during init

If `check_remaining` is enabled, an exception will be raised when data is remaining after decoding

Parameters
----------
data
check_remaining: If enabled, an exception will be raised when data is remaining after decoding

Returns
-------

methods.name: scalecodec.base.ScaleDecoder.encode
documentation:
Encodes the serialized `value` representation of current `ScaleType` to a `ScaleBytes` stream

Parameters
----------
value

Returns
-------
ScaleBytes

documentation:
Implementation of the encoding process

Parameters
----------
value

Returns

ScaleBytes

runtime_config

Base class for all SCALE decoding/encoding

classes.name: scalecodec.types.Compact
documentation: A space efficient type to encoding fixed-width integers

classes.name: scalecodec.types.CompactU32
documentation: Specialized composite implementation for performance improvement

classes.name: scalecodec.types.Option
documentation:
Option is a type that represents an optional value, which can be either Some(value) if a value is present or None
if it is absent, allowing for safe handling of null or missing values without causing runtime errors.

classes.name: scalecodec.types.Bytes
documentation: A variable collection of bytes, stored as an `Vec<u8>`

classes.name: scalecodec.types.U8
documentation: Unsigned 8-bit int type, encoded in little-endian (LE) format

classes.name: scalecodec.types.U16
documentation: Unsigned 16-bit int type, encoded in little-endian (LE) format

classes.name: scalecodec.types.U32
documentation: Unsigned 32-bit int type, encoded in little-endian (LE) format

classes.name: scalecodec.types.U64
documentation: Unsigned 64-bit int type, encoded in little-endian (LE) format

classes.name: scalecodec.types.U128
documentation: Unsigned 128-bit int type, encoded in little-endian (LE) format

classes.name: scalecodec.types.U256
documentation: Unsigned 256-bit int type, encoded in little-endian (LE) format

classes.name: scalecodec.types.I8
documentation: Signed 8-bit int type, encoded in little-endian (LE) format

classes.name: scalecodec.types.I16
documentation: Signed 16-bit int type, encoded in little-endian (LE) format

classes.name: scalecodec.types.I32
documentation: Signed 32-bit int type, encoded in little-endian (LE) format

classes.name: scalecodec.types.I64
documentation: Signed 64-bit int type, encoded in little-endian (LE) format

classes.name: scalecodec.types.I128
documentation: Signed 128-bit int type, encoded in little-endian (LE) format

classes.name: scalecodec.types.I256
documentation: Signed 256-bit int type, encoded in little-endian (LE) format

classes.name: scalecodec.types.H160
documentation: Fixed-size uninterpreted hash type with 20 bytes (160 bits) size.

classes.name: scalecodec.types.H256
documentation: Fixed-size uninterpreted hash type with 32 bytes (256 bits) size.

classes.name: scalecodec.types.H512
documentation: Fixed-size uninterpreted hash type with 64 bytes (512 bits) size.

classes.name: scalecodec.types.Struct
documentation: A struct is a composite data type that groups together zero or more values with various types into a single object

classes.name: scalecodec.types.Set
documentation:
The Set type in Substrate is an unordered collection that contains unique elements.
It is implemented using a binary search tree, allowing for efficient insertion, removal, and lookup operations.

classes.name: scalecodec.types.Era

methods.name: scalecodec.types.Era.is_immortal
documentation: Returns true if the era is immortal, false if mortal.

methods.name: scalecodec.types.Era.birth
documentation:
Gets the block number of the start of the era given, with `current`
as the reference block number for the era, normally included as part
of the transaction.

methods.name: scalecodec.types.Era.death
documentation:
Gets the block number of the first block at which the era has ended.

If the era is immortal, 2**64 - 1 (the maximum unsigned 64-bit integer) is returned.

An Era represents a range of blocks in which a transaction is allowed to be
executed.

An Era may either be "immortal", in which case the transaction is always valid,
or "mortal", in which case the transaction has a defined start block and period
in which it is valid.

classes.name: scalecodec.types.Bool
documentation: Boolean type

classes.name: scalecodec.types.GenericAccountId
documentation: An SS58 formatted representation of an account

classes.name: scalecodec.types.GenericEthereumAccountId
documentation: Representation of an Ethereum address, internally a `H160`

classes.name: scalecodec.types.GenericAccountIndex
documentation:
The AccountIndex type Substrate represents an account index, which is a unique identifier assigned
to an account in the Substrate runtime.

classes.name: scalecodec.types.Vec
documentation:
A Vec in RUST is a dynamically resizable array that can hold a sequence of elements of the same type, allowing for
efficient random access and insertion or removal of elements at the end of the vector.

classes.name: scalecodec.types.BoundedVec
documentation:
BoundedVec is a fixed-size vector used in Substrate, with a maximum number of elements that can be added.
It is used in cases where a fixed-size buffer is required, such as in the case of the transaction input/output
limit in the Substrate runtime.

classes.name: scalecodec.types.BitVec
documentation:
A BitVec that represents an array of bits. The bits are however stored encoded. The difference between this
and a normal Bytes would be that the length prefix indicates the number of bits encoded, not the bytes

classes.name: scalecodec.types.Enum
documentation: A fixed number of variants, each mutually exclusive and potentially implying a further value or series of values.

classes.name: scalecodec.types.Conviction
documentation:
Conviction represents a measure of the degree of confidence or trust that a stakeholder has in a particular proposal
in the Substrate runtime. It is used to determine the voting power of a stakeholder for a particular proposal,
with higher conviction leading to more voting power.

methods.name: scalecodec.types.GenericBlock.process
documentation: TODO implement generic block type

classes.name: scalecodec.types.OpaqueCall
documentation: A Bytes representation of a `Call`, without having to decode the contents

classes.name: scalecodec.types.FixedLengthArray
documentation: Fixed sized array of specified subtype e.g. [u8; 8]

classes.name: scalecodec.types.GenericMultiAddress
documentation: MultiAddress is a type used in the Substrate runtime to represent an address that can be expressed in multiple ways, such as an account ID, a public key, or a human-readable name, allowing for flexible addressing and storage of data.

classes.name: scalecodec.types.GenericMetadataAll
documentation:
Enum that contains a Metadata version.

E.g.  `{"V14": MetadataV14}`

classes.name: scalecodec.types.GenericMetadataVersioned
documentation: Tuple that contains a backwards compatible MetadataAll type

methods.name: scalecodec.types.GenericVariant.get_param_info
documentation:
Generates a dictionary of all possible params with their decomposition information

Returns
-------
dict

methods.name: scalecodec.types.ScaleInfoStorageEntryMetadata.get_param_info
documentation:
Return a type decomposition how to format parameters for current storage function

Returns
-------
list

methods.name: scalecodec.types.GenericRuntimeCallDefinition.get_param_info
documentation:
Return a type decomposition how to format parameters for current storage function

Returns
-------
list

classes.name: scalecodec.types.GenericExtrinsic
documentation:
The Extrinsic type is used to send transactions from an account to the Substrate runtime, and it can contain
one or more calls to the runtime functions.

classes.name: scalecodec.types.GenericEvent
documentation:
An Event is a type used to represent a runtime event, which is a signal that indicates that a
specific state transition has occurred.

It is implemented as a variant enum that contains different types of events, such as system events, runtime events,
and custom events, each with its own set of fields that describe the event.
